items.q: What is the scope of a local variable?
items.correct: Accessible only within the function/block where it is declared.
items.random_wrong: Stored on a local computer rather than a server.
items.why: Students confuse 'local' with 'on this computer'. In programming, scope refers to the CODE REGION where the variable name resolves - local variables vanish when the function returns.

items.q: What is the main advantage of asymmetric encryption?
items.correct: Secure key exchange without sharing a secret in advance.
items.misconception: It is faster than symmetric encryption.
items.why: Students think asymmetric is just 'better'. It's actually SLOWER than symmetric. Its advantage is solving key distribution - the public key can be shared openly without compromising security.

items.correct: The ability of different classes to respond to the same method call in their own way.
items.misconception: Having multiple classes with the same name.
items.random_wrong: Variables that change type at runtime.
items.why: Students confuse polymorphism with naming. Polymorphism means 'many forms' - a single interface (method signature) behaving differently depending on the object's actual class.
Correct answer: Memoisation. “Tabulation” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: DP requires problems to have overlapping...
items.why: The answer examiners want is: subproblems. “loops” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Time complexity of computing Fibonacci(n) using DP (give k in O(n^k))?
items.why: The real figure is 1 k. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
